summaryText: The service was a powerful time of worship and declaration centered on the victory and transformative power found in Jesus Christ. Through a series of songs and declarations, the congregation celebrated the core truths of the Christian faith: being washed clean from sin, rescued from spiritual death, and given new life. The lyrics repeatedly emphasized that this salvation is not based on human effort but on what Jesus accomplished through His death and resurrection, taking away shame and nailing it to the cross. This leads to a life of freedom, symbolized by 'dancing on the grave' of one's past. The worship affirmed Jesus as the beautiful, powerful, and only worthy name, the miracle-working God who is constantly at work even when He is not seen or felt. The overall message was one of profound gratitude, identity in Christ, and confident hope in His ongoing work and supreme authority.
mainPoints: Salvation through Jesus brings complete cleansing from sin and shame, offering a new, victorious identity.
Jesus Christ is the uniquely beautiful, powerful, and worthy name, above all rivals, deserving of all praise.
God is a miracle-worker who is actively at work in our lives even during times when we cannot see or feel His presence.

Monday.scripture: Ephesians 2:4-5 (NIV) - 'But because of his great love for us, God, who is rich in mercy, made us alive with Christ even when we were dead in transgressions—it is by grace you have been saved.'
Monday.action: Write down one thing from your 'old life' that Christ has rescued you from, and thank Him for it.
Monday.prayer: Lord, thank you for making me alive with Christ. Help me to live today in the freedom of that new life.

Tuesday.scripture: Colossians 2:13-14 (NIV) - 'He forgave us all our sins, having canceled the charge of our legal indebtedness, which stood against us and condemned us; he has taken it away, nailing it to the cross.'
Tuesday.action: When a thought of past failure or shame arises today, consciously say, 'That is nailed to the cross.'
Tuesday.prayer: Jesus, thank you for taking my shame to the cross. I receive your forgiveness and choose to walk in your victory today.

Wednesday.scripture: Philippians 2:9-11 (NIV) - 'Therefore God exalted him to the highest place and gave him the name that is above every name, that at the name of Jesus every knee should bow...'
Wednesday.action: Listen to or sing a worship song that declares the power and beauty of Jesus' name.
Wednesday.prayer: Jesus, you are worthy of all praise. I declare that there is no name higher than yours in my life and in this world.

Thursday.scripture: John 1:29 (NIV) - 'The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, "Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!"'
Thursday.action: Take a moment of silence to reflect on the cost of your salvation.
Thursday.prayer: Lamb of God, I am in awe of your love that took away my sin. I worship you for who you are.

Friday.scripture: Romans 8:11 (NIV) - 'And if the Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead is living in you, he who raised Christ from the dead will also give life to your mortal bodies because of his Spirit who lives in you.'
Friday.action: Acknowledge God's Spirit in you by asking for His guidance in one decision today.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, you are at work in me. Give me life and strength today. Help me to see where you are working.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 77:14 (NIV) - 'You are the God who performs miracles; you display your power among the peoples.'
Saturday.action: Recall and give thanks for one 'miracle'—big or small—you have witnessed in your life or the life of someone you know.
Saturday.prayer: Miracle-working God, I believe you are at work even when I don't see it. I trust you with the situations that need your touch in my life.
